title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Y Vehicles
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit a record of 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles operating driverless.
impact_reason: Direct evidence of modest growth in Austin's unsupervised fleet size enhances scaling momentum by increasing real-world miles and data collection for further unsupervised expansion.

title: Cybercab Volume Production Planned for Late 2026 at Giga Texas
summary: Tesla plans to begin volume production of the Cybercab—a purpose-built, two-seat autonomous vehicle with no steering wheel or pedals—later this year at Giga Texas. This dedicated hardware is intended to power scaled robotaxi deployment.
impact_reason: Mildly positive: hardware production timeline removes a key gating constraint for fleet scaling post-H1 2026, though late-year start limits impact on near-term expansion velocity.

title: Tesla Q1 Earnings Preview Highlights Robotaxi Rollout Widening to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla is expanding its Robotaxi rollout to Dallas and Houston amid Q1 earnings preparations, alongside a planned capex surge to $20B focused on autonomous capabilities.
impact_reason: New city launches in Dallas and Houston directly accelerate unsupervised robotaxi scaling by expanding geofences and active markets in Texas.

title: Tesla Q1 2026 Update Details Robotaxi Expansion Groundwork to Additional US Metros
summary: Tesla's official Q1 2026 Update states continued progress on Robotaxi service expansion to additional major U.S. metros through testing and permitting activities.
impact_reason: Official confirmation of groundwork for new metro expansions supports future scaling velocity by advancing regulatory and testing prerequisites, though lacks specific timelines or fleet sizes.

title: Tesla Plans to Double Austin Robotaxi Fleet to ~60 by December
summary: Elon Musk announced plans to double the Austin Robotaxi fleet from 29-30 current vehicles to about 60 next month, far short of the prior 500-vehicle end-2025 target. All current Austin rides remain supervised with safety drivers.
impact_reason: Promises fleet growth but remains supervised and well below targets, resulting in neutral net effect on unsupervised scaling velocity in Austin.

title: Tesla Doubles Austin Robotaxi Geofence to 171 sq mi, Adds Airport
summary: Tesla expanded its Austin Robotaxi service area by 86 square miles to 171 square miles total, the third and largest expansion since launch, now including Austin airport and surpassing Waymo's area. This follows prior expansions from 20 to 85 square miles.
impact_reason: Doubling the Austin geofence to over 170 square miles in rapid succession significantly increases the operational area for unsupervised rides, enabling more rides and data collection to accelerate fleet scaling.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, adding compact geofence areas in each city alongside ongoing operations in Austin. This triples Tesla's active Texas hubs, building on months of unsupervised rides in Austin.
impact_reason: Launching unsupervised service in two major new Texas cities directly triples the state footprint and demonstrates rapid geographic scaling beyond Austin, aligning with H1 2026 multi-city rollout plans.

title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

title: TechCrunch: Tesla Brings Robotaxi Service to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla expanded unsupervised robotaxi operations to Dallas and Houston, now operating in three Texas cities after Austin launch, with video confirmation of no human drivers or monitors.
impact_reason: Confirms operational expansion in Texas, concentrating resources and regulatory learnings in a favorable state to boost unsupervised scaling speed across Austin and new areas.
